questions.question: Given a Naive Bayes classifier with 3 classes (A, B, C) and a feature set {x1, x2, x3}, determine the likelihood of a new instance with feature values (2, 3, 4) given that the prior probabilities are A = 0.4, B = 0.3, C = 0.3. Assume add-1 smoothing for the likelihoods. What is the value of the likelihood of the new instance given class A?

An English sentence 'The dog runs quickly' is classified into one of the three categories 'Sports', 'Politics', 'Entertainment'. The prior probabilities for the categories are P(S) = 0.2, P(P) = 0.3, P(E) = 0.5. Calculate the likelihood of the sentence given the categories using Naive Bayes with add-1 smoothing for the likelihoods. What is the value of the likelihood of the sentence given category 'Sports'?

A text dataset is classified into one of the two categories 'Positive' or 'Negative'. The prior probabilities are P(P) = 0.6 and P(N) = 0.4. The feature set is {x1, x2}, where x1 is the number of words in the sentence and x2 is the frequency of the word 'love'. Assume the likelihoods are calculated using Naive Bayes with add-1 smoothing. What is the value of the likelihood of the sentence with x1 = 5 and x2 = 2 given category 'Positive'?
